title: santalum insulare
intro: Santalum insulare, also known as the Lord Howe Island sandalwood, is a unique and endangered plant species native to Lord Howe Island in Australia. It is a type of tree that belongs to the sandalwood family and is prized for its fragrant wood and essential oils. The medicinal properties of Santalum insulare include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its active compounds, including santalol and alpha-santalol. The health benefits of this plant include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of depression, and it can be prepared as an essential oil, salve, or tincture for various herbal applications.
